title: Why I yawn all the time - Request

transcript.text: Here was a video request.
Just Dano, go.
You go, and I'll even show.
Video request, why are you yawning all the time?
Also, not just in this video.
Yeah, yeah, I do.
I yawn all the time in my videos.
I'm not a yawner in general.
Like, you know, if you were to be my roommate or whatever.
Here I go.
You would never see me yawn.
I don't yawn.
So it's kind of a mystery of why I yawn.
You know, I didn't start yawning all the time until I started doing videos.
It is kind of a mystery of why I do it.
A lot of people get pissed off at me like I'm doing it on purpose.
I guess making the sound can be annoying.
So I should yawn and not make a sound and then not open my mouth in front of people.
Because I'm not used to looking inside people's mouths.
But, like, I think I do it because, well, I go most of the time not talking.
So if I start talking all of a sudden, I'm doing movements that my body is not used to.
And I'm moving around my jaw muscles.
And 80% of the blood inside of your brain passes through your jaws.
And so that's why you yawn.
You yawn to open up those blood vessels to get oxygen into your brain.
Increase that circulation.
And, you know, people yawn when they're tired.
Maybe because when you're tired, you just don't get as much oxygen to your brain or something.
I haven't done the research on it.
But this is just a video I'm doing off the top of my head.
But I think with me is I do videos and I access, like, all parts of my mind when I do a video.
And I do pretty good.
I'm pretty happy with how imaginative I am when I do a video.
You know, they're all spontaneous.
I don't plan my videos out.
But sometimes, you know, like somebody gave me a request about what are libraries going to be like 10 to 15 years.
I would want to do that video off the top of my head.
That's something I might want to sit down and think about a little bit.
But, you know, other than that, I pretty much did my videos off the top of my head.
And I'm using my imagination.
I think my brain kind of, like, is all, is like, is now, like, in full gear and trying to go as hard as it can.
And it's basically telling my body, you know, give me oxygen.
Give me some fuel here.
And so I start yawning.
You know, I start yawning because I'm using my brain.
So I am more likely to yawn if I'm tired than if I'm awake.
And, you know, I think sometimes I'm tired.
A lot of the time I'm tired.
I have insomnia, so it's kind of hard.
But I'm the kind of person who could be totally exhausted and still have insomnia.
So I think, yeah, sometimes also it's being tired.
Doing videos when you're tired.
I do a lot of videos late at night.
Like right now it's like midnight.
What time is it exactly?
Yeah.
Oh, my God.
It's almost 1.
Fuck.
And, yeah, so, you know, I'm tired because it's late.
And, yeah, what else?
Yeah, it's weird that I'm the only person who does it.
I remember in college I yawned a lot because I remember I was in this French class
And this lady was like, don't yawn, don't yawn.
You're bored.
Don't yawn.
And other teachers and stuff.
And I didn't say anything.
And I'm like, wow, I'm not tired.
I'm just yawning.
I don't know.
Um, so, yeah, I guess my brain is just like, give me more, give me more.
Somebody told me that.
It's indicative of some kind of sickness.
But I can't remember what they meant, but I can't remember what they meant, what they said.
Anoflaxia or something like that.
Um, I don't, huh, I don't know, man.
I don't think so.
I think I'm perfectly healthy.
Um, but, you know, oxygen wise, uh, I'll see if I yawn as much when I'm in shape.
Because when you're in shape, your circulation is a lot better.
Um, and maybe I'll have good enough of a circulation to get that oxygen in my brain and I won't be yawning.
That'd be an interesting thing to find out.
Um, but, uh, it only happens when I'm talking on YouTube videos.
Because I don't go around in my daily life yawning.
I never yawn, pretty much.
Only when I start doing videos.
And sometimes I can't stop yawning when I do a video.
It's really weird.
But, uh, it's kind of a mystery.
Okay.
Peace out.
Peace out.
